title: Post-Init Doctor + Release Flow
objective: Add a post-init validation path and a release contract so the installer can be published and trusted as a versioned entrypoint.
description: A one-command installer still fails the plug-and-play bar if users cannot validate what it generated or if maintainers do not have a documented release flow. This slice adds the post-init doctor experience and the package release contract needed to ship the installer responsibly.
must: Add a post-init validation command or mode that checks the generated Quiver project and reports actionable next steps
Document the release and versioning flow for publishing the installer surface
Extend CI with at least one smoke check that validates the packaged installer can be exercised before release
acceptance: After installation, a user can run the documented doctor flow to confirm the scaffold is ready for slice work
Maintainers have a documented and repeatable release flow for versioning and publishing the installer
CI proves the installer surface is still usable before a release is cut
tests: Packaged CLI smoke test in CI
assumptions: The installer release flow remains npm-first
The post-init doctor should validate the same contract enforced by Quiver's workflow scripts
